title: Spicy Syrian Cacao Bars
description: Bitter, crumbly bars infused with Thai red curry paste and cacao nibs, offering an energizing, kosher-friendly snack that melds Syrian heat and Latin cacao.
instructions: Preheat oven to 350 °F (if baking) or simply assemble for a no‑bake version.
In a heat‑proof bowl, combine melted coconut oil, coconut milk, honey, and vanilla extract; whisk until smooth.
Stir in Thai red curry paste, ground ginger, turmeric, sea salt, lime zest, and lime juice until fully incorporated.
Fold in cacao nibs and almond flour, mixing until the mixture is uniformly crumbly and well‑bound.
Transfer the dough to a greased 8‑inch square pan, pressing firmly to create an even layer. If baking, bake for 15–20 min until set; if no‑bake, chill in refrigerator for 30 min.
Remove from pan, cut into 12 bars, and store in an airtight container in the fridge for up to one week.
